programme.desc: A retired American football star is murdered, but his widow jeopardises Alexx's investigation by seizing the body, determined to have it cryonically frozen before an autopsy can be performed. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: A man bumps into his apparently deceased wife, the woman he served seven years in prison for murdering. However, when she goes missing, he is once again arrested under suspicion of killing her in an act of revenge(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: After Della reads the diary of a dead woman, Perry gets involved in a bizarre case with an eccentric millionaire and a kleptomaniac gorilla.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team rushes to return Walter to Earth when he is accidentally launched into space, where he hallucinates about Paige as his oxygen runs low. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker faces a man who seems to be the reincarnation of a Native American witch doctor, and time-travels to the 19th century to right past wrongs and stop history repeating itself(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team search for a boy from Ralph's class who went missing during a field trip, and Toby gets closer to uncovering the identity of Happy's husband(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man is garrotted beside a hotel pool surrounded by dozens of other sun-worshippers, yet none of them witnesses the attack because the killer struck during a solar eclipse, when all eyes were trained on the sky. Even more baffling, the first DNA evidence suggests a human-wolf mutation was present at the time of the attack(n)

programme.desc: A terminally ill patient begs for Dr Sloan's help. Ten years before, he was wrongly convicted of his wife's murder - now he wants to find the culprit and prove his innocence before he dies. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark takes on a temporary assignment as a physician on a cruise ship, where he finds himself investigating the killing of a passenger with more than his fair share of enemies. Dick Van Dyke stars(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects Gibbs's arch-nemesis is behind an explosion at a summit on global terror. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Fornell struggles to deal with the fatal shooting of his ex-wife. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Joe Spano(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A covert identity created for one of Gibbs' undercover cases 20 years previously resurfaces after a murder victim is found to be using the alias. Meanwhile, Bishop becomes anxious at the thought of having to work incognito in the future. Starring Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter is forced to enlist the help of former troubled team member and genius Mark Collins when a nuclear reactor at a Los Angeles power plant is on the verge of a cataclysmic meltdown. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members must pass a mandated psychiatric evaluation or face being disbanded after they are accused of destroying a priceless work of art. Meanwhile, Ralph is reluctant to go to his school's Halloween party. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. He soon discovers a link to an affair between the school priest and the wife of the headmaster - a scandal that led to a woman's disappearance years before. Caribbean crime drama, starring Ben Miller and Sara Martins, with Mona Hammond, Michael Maloney and Nicholas Farrell(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopen a 1992 abduction case when the woman who disappeared turns up alive and well, claiming she was never kidnapped in the first place. Halford suspects the apparent victim's mother of faking the incident to get her ex-husband to pay the ransom. Drama, starring Amanda Redman, James Bolam and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.desc: Jardine and Reid probe the apparently motiveless murder of a well-to-do businessman, focusing their investigations on a shady matrimonial agency with a roster of female clients from the former Soviet Union. Glasgow-set crime drama, starring Blythe Duff and James Macpherson(n)

programme.desc: A death on a local farm is blamed on a Kembleford doctor through a series of poison pen letters, and Father Brown must try to find out who sent them to save his friend from the gallows. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A day out at a local hill climb track ends with one of the drivers being killed. Father Brown persists with his personal investigation, and an infuriated Sullivan resolves to put an end to his meddling for good. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: During a performance of Hamlet, young cast member Ben Faulkner collapses and dies. Initially the detectives think Ben died of natural causes, until Mike uncovers a complex web of ego-driven dissension among the troupe and follows his nose to the real cause of death(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: After a harsh anti-immigrant law comes into force, Murdoch and Brackenreid find themselves dealing with the fallout for its enforcers and the Greek immigrants it targets(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam suspects a riot is in the offing when a football fan is killed. Knowing the problem will escalate as time goes by, the detective becomes determined to nip the violence in the bud, so he, Gene and Annie go undercover in a pub to catch the culprits. However, he realises the derby match that will be the focus of the conflict is the last one he went to with his dad when he was four years old(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam hears his mother's voice saying goodbye, and worries that doctors in 2006 are about to turn off his life support system. When a hostage situation crops up, he becomes convinced that resolving the crisis without a single casualty will cause him to wake up in his own time. However, Gene is determined to go in with all guns blazing - putting everyone's safety at risk. John Simm stars(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Customs seize a massive heroin shipment and Kavanagh agrees to prosecute the importer, a self-made man with all the trappings of success, including a yacht and a villa in Portugal. Unfortunately, he also has a staff of hired heavies who make the mistake of threatening Lizzie in a bid to get him off the hook - thus setting the scene for a bitterly personal courtroom wrangle. John Thaw and Lisa Harrow star(n)

programme.title: Doctor Finlay

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Having discovered Irene Radford is back, Gina is determined to put her old enemy behind bars once and for all - and the two are soon face to face again when an intruder at the Radford house turns out to be Irene herself, who claims she has returned because she is critically ill with cancer. However, the police have got wind that she is plotting one final raid before retiring from a life of crime for good, so Phil is tasked to monitor a prison meeting between Irene and her son David, where the top brass are hoping he'll glean valuable information about the job. Unfortunately, the philandering DS is easily distracted by an old flame working at the prison and misses half the conversation - so when the raid gets under way, it soon becomes clear they've been duped, and a senior officer ends up staring down the barrel of Irene's gun. Back at the station, Honey is upset to learn that Amber has taken the 'shy and sensitive' Steve Hunter's virginity, while Sam seems to be warming to Phil. Is ro(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following the Radford family's botched attempt to spring David from prison, Irene drives off with Gina at gunpoint and forces the inspector to let her colleagues know she means business. If they don't give up the chase, she won't hesitate to pull the trigger. Soon, Gina finds herself alone with her enemy, and as they begin to talk, the two of them realise they understand one another better than they could possibly have imagined. However, when Irene orders the shooting of Jack Gaunt, it becomes clear Gina is still in very real danger. Meanwhile, Honey has it out with Steve, disappointed that he had sex with Amber, and accusing him of being as bad as his brother. Indeed, Phil is having his fair share of problems as Sam discovers that his New Year's invitation followed hard on the heels of his dalliance with the prison liaison officer. Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham, Lisa Maxwell and Scott Maslen star(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Staff shortages cause tension in CPU, with Ramani bearing the brunt as she struggles to cope with an increased workload - and when an outside source brings the matter to Adam's attention, the situation becomes hugely political, only serving to aggravate the already volatile relationship between Jack and Neil. By way of an immediate solution, Jack assigns Terry to help Ramani with the case of a man suspected of being a paedophile - but Ramani is worried about Terry's ability to deal with such a task given his own traumatic background. Meanwhile, the rest of the relief let their hair down at Lance's stag party - but when the guest of honour nips outside for a breath of fresh air, he is suddenly attacked from behind by a group who put a bag over his head and bundle him into the back of a van. It could just be a stag night prank - or it could be something more serious. Guest starring Annabelle Apsion and Colm O'Maonlai(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lance is missing on his wedding day, as friends try to 'cure' him of his homosexuality. Terry tries to prove his suspicions that brother Ben is a child abuser, while no-nonsense new DC Jo Masters arrives at Sun Hill to defuse the tension between Jack and Neil - but proves to be their worst nightmare(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a battered wife in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a women's refuge. Hetty's quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: When her niece's son is charged with possession of an ecstasy tablet, suspicions remain that he has been framed, so Hetty goes undercover in his school as a cafeteria worker to clear his name. Patricia Routledge stars(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Colour Blind
programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is gripped by an outbreak of dysentery, but that's the least of mum-to-be Marie Amos's worries when her husband Tony is arrested for gross indecency in the gentlemen's toilets and faces a criminal hearing. Nurse Crane meets Dolores McEvoy, a pregnant Irish woman who is clearly malnourished - but when she visits the woman's home, it turns out she has given a false address and has disappeared. Trixie is delighted when Tom asks her to oversee her first official church duty since their engagement, while at Nonnatus House, Fred tackles a rat infestation - only for Sister Monica Joan to work against him. With guest appearances by Cara Theobold (Downton Abbey) and Richard Fleeshman (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Winifred's eyes are opened to Poplar's seedy underworld when she cares for expectant prostitute Bridget Cole. Horrified by the young woman's lack of awareness, she draws on her teaching experience, and with help from Sgt Noakes sets out to promote safe sex to all local working girls. Barbara struggles to understand why expectant father Frank Robbins would pressure his wife to bear him a son, while Sister Monica Joan is feeling sidelined - until she finds herself unexpectedly assisting Shelagh with an emergency birth. Trixie invites herself along to a meeting between Tom and the Bishop - but things get tense when the idea of the couple moving to Newcastle is raised(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw(n)

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh stars as the world-weary Swedish detective in a fourth and final series of adaptations of Henning Mankell's novels. Wallander travels to Cape Town to investigate the disappearance of a Swedish woman. He uncovers a complex conspiracy behind the mystery, and is also confronted with a nation that has reached a turning point in its history(n)

programme.title: Gregory's Girl (1981)
programme.desc: A gawky teenager is dismayed to be relegated to goalkeeper in the school football team - but his thoughts turn to romance once he meets the female striker who takes his place. However, his awkward nature proves an obstacle to winning her over, despite the sage advice of his worldly-wise little sister. Comedy, starring John Gordon Sinclair, Dee Hepburn, Jake D'Arcy, Clare Grogan and Robert Buchanan(n)

programme.desc: Mari is willing to help everyone, often giving up her free time in order to ensure that others have a break. However, one day, Mari learns that sometimes it is better to say 'no' than let everyone - including herself - down(n)

programme.desc: The Olobobs hear a beautiful snoof song, but find it impossible to get near the special snoof as every time they approach it the snoof flies away. Can Twitchybeard help them learn more about this mysterious creature?(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

Natasha also steps aboard a narrowboat antiques shop moored on a canal, while Raj rummages in the den of a pair of foxes and emerges with perhaps his most unpredictable purchase to date(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is convinced Mick and Shirley are plotting against her, so she sneaks out and confides her fears to Sharon, who urges the troubled landlady to fight for her marriage. programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 400m gold medallist Christine Ohuruogu, Cricket World Cup-winner Mark Wood, Sheffield United striker Billy Sharp and Great Britain swimming star Fran Halsall join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Winkle gatecrashes Gary's plans for a family day out at an indoor mini golf course in celebration of Big Gary's birthday. Meanwhile, Denise takes a shine to the crockery in the restaurant and Terri sticks her oar into Gary's friend John the Butcher's date. She knows that John is already seeing one of her friends, so righteously informs the date that the man she is having dinner with is a 'class A wrong un'. Comedy, starring Tom Davis, Laura Checkley and Camille Coduri(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Hollywood star Mark Ruffalo, who is currently appearing in new legal thriller Dark Waters. Friends star David Schwimmer and British comedian and writer Nick Mohammed discuss their new spy comedy Intelligence. Episodes star Tamsin Greig also appears to talk about her role in Julian Fellowes' new period drama, Belgravia. Plus, there's music from singer-songwriter Alicia Keys, who performs her new single Underdog, while more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The remaining queens are split into two teams for a girl group battle. With the help of Grammy-nominated singer and producer MNEK, the two newly formed groups record vocals and perform their own versions of the same song. Little Mix's Jade Thirlwall joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel(n)

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: After a positive first couple of weeks, the temptation of unhealthy treats and alcohol is threatening to undo the group's progress, so Tom shares a series of recipes to help the volunteers with their temptations for unhealthy treats. He starts with his own take on Asian crispy duck pancakes, before demonstrating how to make fudge chocolate brownies which are lower in calories. Later, Tom visits Lauren in her home, where he gives her a lesson in making prawn and avocado rice paper rolls which contain lots of fresh, healthy ingredients(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from Newark with experts Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell. While in town, Charlie pays a visit to the National Civil War Centre(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists prepare for another two challenges but first there's a surprise as, for one week only, Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge joins the judges in Angela Hartnett's place. Then it's down to business as Mary Berry asks them to cook their ultimate family pie and Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish using a root vegetable usually served on the side. As usual, those who least impress the judges compete in the eliminator, a recipe close to Tom's heart that he expects to be executed perfectly. Presented by Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). John Inverdale presents all the action from Murrayfield, where both teams are looking to recover from respective defeats against Ireland and France. Scotland could count themselves somewhat unfortunate to return from Dublin on the losing side, but England's dismal start against France cost them any chance of victory. The sides shared the spoils in a thrilling 38-38 draw when they met in the final match of last season's competition. The Scots staged an improbable fightback from 31-7 down at half-time, with only George Ford's try and conversion in the final play of the game denying them a first win at Twickenham in 36 years, though the draw was enough for them to retain the Calcutta Cup following their 25-13 win here in 2018. With analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott, Andy Nicol and John Barclay, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Chris Paterson(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining 10 acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie struggles to deal with recent events in the emergency department, and Connie finds it increasingly difficult to hide her emotions, which could bring her and Archie closer together. Meanwhile, Rash finds himself in the middle of an intense and complex case when two friends are brought to the emergency department for treatment(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him and he locks himself away in a cupboard, so it is up to Agnes, Father Quinn and best man Buster to try to calm him down. Meanwhile, Rory's secret is revealed to his mother. Bawdy comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Paddy Houlihan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: Free State of Jones (2016)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting for the Confederacy during the American Civil War grows disillusioned by the rebel cause and deserts, returning to his Mississippi home. He unites a force of struggling farmers and escaped slaves into a militia, fighting to turn their county into an independent state, free from the rule of both sides. Fact-based drama, starring Matthew McConaughey and Gugu Mbatha-Raw(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience at Oasis Academy MediaCityUK in Salford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, his wife Erin and their two children sample life in Melbourne for a week, hoping the move would improve their work-life balance and quality of family time(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks at faith and autism, meeting a minister with Asperger's syndrome and hearing from members of the world's first performing autistic adult choir(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes property hunting in rural Wiltshire with a couple who have £950,000 to buy a family home with land, where they and their young children can enjoy a self-sufficient lifestyle. Jules also takes time out to find out about the county's glove-making heritage and sees some famous examples made for famous figures(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Italy (Kick-off 3.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the concluding match from the second round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. The hosts will be in confident mood after their impressive performance against England in their opening match, while Italy were comfortably routed by Wales. The French prevailed 25-14 in Italy last year to extend their winning run against the Azzurri in this competition to six matches. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede, Sergio Parisse and Joe Worsley, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Paul O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: From ancient Syria to present day Sheffield and out into the wilds of space, something is stalking the Doctor and her friends. As Graham, Yaz and Ryan return home to see friends and family, they find themselves haunted by very different experiences. Who is the figure calling from beyond the stars for help, and what are the fearsome Chagaskas terrorising Aleppo in 1380? To find the answers, the Doctor and her companions must embark on a mission that forces them to face their darkest fears(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: At the Turners, Shelagh receives a phone call from the Hong Kong adoption agency, which has the alarming news that someone from May's past has arrived in the UK and wants to see the little girl. Meanwhile, Valerie's cousin Maureen is heavily pregnant and hoping her baby will be born on Thursday, the same date as her father and grandfather. However, her due date isn't so soon and keen to bring on the birth, Maureen takes a big risk by secretly taking castor oil - with potentially disastrous consequences(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Widowed antique dealer Mark Easterbrook investigates when his name appears on a list found in the shoe of a dead woman, and his enquiries lead him to the home of three spinsters in Much Deeping. The village is a place of old traditions, macabre rituals and strange beliefs, a place of witches, curses and spells, and as more deaths occur Mark starts to fear for his own life and sanity. First of a two-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Sheffield United v Bournemouth at Bramall Lane. West Ham were looking to avenge a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of the Citizens on the opening weekend of the season, while Sheffield United were aiming to continue their impressive campaign back in the top flight by inflicting more misery upon the struggling Cherries(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, featuring Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Brighton & Hove Albion at Damson Park(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the second round of fixtures, featuring France v Italy at Stade Beaublanc, Scotland v England at Scotstoun and Ireland v Wales at Energia Park(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Krystyn Onaivi wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia. However, husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perth is arranged to give the couple the chance to look at the homes, jobs and lifestyle on offer 'Down Under'. Whether the experience will unite or drive a wedge between the pair remains to be seen. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram go antique shopping in Norfolk, where the items that take their fancy include an unusual gavel, a pair of polo horses and a vintage train set(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the penultimate challenge, the four remaining chefs are challenged to create a single exceptional dish, inspired by a place that is special to them, as they fight to secure their place in the final three. After one contestant is sent home, the three remaining chefs travel to Lisbon to experience cooking alongside the acclaimed chef José Avillez. First, they take a trip to an historical food market, where they find ingredients to create their own dish for Chef José to taste, before joining José's brigade of chefs during a packed lunchtime service(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Keith and Greer, from the west coast of Scotland, enlist the help of local property expert Ali in the Costa del Sol to find a perfect rental home for them and their two dogs. But will they find what they're looking for and make the move south for winter? Gloria Hunniford narrates.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team looks at stories linked to estuaries around the UK. Nick Crane heads to the Firth of Forth to investigate the 'soliton', a rare type of wave that appears to travel endlessly without losing energy or breaking up, while Miranda Krestovnikoff reveals how salmon avoid death by dehydration as they migrate to saltwater. Tessa Dunlop examines a Victorian system on the Thames that relied on the power of the tide to wash London's sewage out to sea, while Mark Horton learns about the struggle to build a rail tunnel under the Severn estuary to connect England and Wales, a challenge that was finally accomplished in 1886. Folk singer June Tabor visits the Somerset village of Brean to explore the superstitions surrounding the burial of sailors who died in shipwrecks(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by outspoken actress Shirley MacLaine, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Love Is a Many Splendored Thing (1955)
programme.desc: A married American news correspondent reporting on the Korean War from his base in Hong Kong falls in love with a widowed Eurasian doctor - but he is unable to get a divorce and the couple find themselves forced to keep their relationship a secret. Romantic drama based on the memoirs of Han Suyin, with William Holden, Jennifer Jones and Torin Thatcher(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston-upon-Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall hunt for objects to take to auction. Presenter Paul Martin heads to Sewerby Hall in Bridlington to learn the life story of Hull girl Amy Johnson, the first woman to fly solo from England to Australia(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Approaching Vietnamese capital Hanoi aboard the Unification Railway, Michael passes the famous street where the locomotive passes within centimetres of the houses either side of the tracks. Checking into its famous Metropole Hotel, he discovers a bunker where guests sheltered from American bombardment during the Vietnam War. At a former Soviet factory that once supplied military uniforms to the North Vietnamese army and Viet Cong, Michael discovers a progressive new manufacturer providing sportswear to the United States Olympic team. He later views the limestone islets and turquoise waters of Ha Long Bay(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the ordinary men and women of the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, who are ready to answer their pager's call and race to the rescue of those in trouble, 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, whatever the weather. Using footage shot on the crews' own cameras, the series captures the unpredictable work of the RNLI in unique detail(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Adam Henson and Steve Brown team up with antique experts David Harper and Roo Irvine for a trip around the rolling Cotswold Hills, hoping to find items that will make the biggest profit at auction. The competing duo also explore the area's history, with Adam discovering the vital work a charity played saving horses during the Great War and Steve taking a trip down a canal to learn how a little boat save hundreds of lives at Dunkirk(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Mehreen Baig and a team of experts explore four spectacular coastlines linked by the Irish Sea, and meet the people who call them home. Each programme features people local to each region, as they fully immerse in the coastal way of life, from tackling unique jobs to experiencing magical close encounters with wildlife. The first focuses on the Merseyside coast, which stretches from Sefton Sands in the north to the Wirral in the south, via the port city of Liverpool(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'Quantity' and 'Quality', inviting Bridget Christie, Joe Lycett and James Acaster to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores how Scotland's landscape has shaped the nation's history. He begins on a trek from Aberdeen to Oban, meeting a hermit called Jake and exploring his very unique treehouse. He then visits the community of Findhorn, finding out how this remote area of barren sand dunes became transformed into a thriving village whose main aim is to be environmentally, economically and socially self-sustaining. Frankie also views one of the most stunning glens in the Highlands, giving his perspective on the Glencoe massacre of 1692, before going deep underground to visit the Cruachan Power Station(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard leads a debate on why people consume so much sensationalised death in movies, crime fiction and TV drama, while having a reluctance to speaking about real pain and loss. Plus, how comedy can help people to come to terms with grief and their own mortality(n)

programme.title: Southside with You (2016)
programme.desc: Biographical drama chronicling the afternoon in the summer of 1989, when the future 44th President of the United States Barack Obama and Michelle Robinson went on their first date across the South Side of Chicago. Starring Tika Sumpter and Parker Sawyers(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme meets some of the growing number of people living in temporary accommodation. With a shortage of council-owned properties, and a desire to keep people out of bed and breakfasts, local authorities are increasingly turning to the private sector for help. In 2013, a change in the law meant companies could buy up and convert old office blocks into homes without planning permission. Reporter Callum Tulley explores life for residents living in these converted spaces(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: What connects a missing astronaut, birds behaving strangely in Peru and a US naval officer who washes up on a Madagascar beach? The Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham split up to investigate these mysterious events across 21st-century planet Earth, but as the mystery deepens and lives are put at risk, they face a deadly race against time to save humanity. Guest starring Warren Brown and Matthew McNulty, with Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh, Tosin Cole and Mandip Gill(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate the spookier side of nature, including a tree that bleeds, a fungus that likes to eat ant brains and a flesh-chewing fly(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall finds out about deadly animals in the UK, discovering dragonflies are not only skilled killers in the air, but also terrorise the underwater world as larvae(n)

programme.title: How Earth Made Us
programme.desc: Having documented the planetary forces that have shaped the development of civilisations, Iain Stewart discovers how this relationship has been reciprocated. He examines how the advent of farming prevented the world from slipping into a new ice age, and demonstrates why humanity unwittingly caused a catastrophic mudslide in Indonesia(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the grand final. Robots Aftershock, Eruption, Ironside 3, Concussion and Carbide have all made it through to this stage, and will be joined in the arena by an unconfirmed wild card selected by judges Professor Sethu Vijayakumar, Dr Lucy Rogers and Professor Noel Sharkey. The teams must fight through two group battles and a mini-league before this year's champion is announced(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Mehreen Baig and a team of experts explore four spectacular coastlines linked by the Irish Sea, and meet the people who call them home. Each programme features people local to each region, as they fully immerse in the coastal way of life, from tackling unique jobs to experiencing magical close encounters with wildlife. The first focuses on the Merseyside coast, which stretches from Sefton Sands in the north to the Wirral in the south, via the port city of Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef takes a gastronomic road trip to Istanbul through the countries of the former Byzantine Empire - a melting pot of East and West. His journey begins in the city of Venice, and on the idyllic Greek island of Symi he cooks some of his favourite Venetian dishes including seafood risotto, tiramisu and gnocchi with spider crab(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the pride of African lions featured in Africa's Giant Killers: Natural World, which detailed a clash between drought-weakened elephants and hungry big cats in the northern stretches of the Kalahari Desert in Botswana. This follow-up rejoins the pride at the start of the rainy season, when the departure of the elephants brings a whole host of new problems for the lions. The males begin to turn against each other, and one inexperienced lioness places her cubs in mortal danger as a rival group challenges the area's resident lions. Wild fires caused by lightning devastate the landscape, and as the dust begins to settle, the pride must once again face off with the elephants or risk starvation. Narrated by Imogen Stubbs(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the careers of some of the most successful directors in movie history, including John Ford, Frank Capra, Sidney Lumet, George Cukor, Billy Wilder and Cecil B DeMille, among others. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Three Coins in the Fountain (1954)
programme.desc: Three American women working as secretaries at the US embassy in Rome throw coins into the city's Trevi Fountain in the hope of meeting the men of their dreams. Their wishes are answered in the shape of an Italian translator, a successful writer and a debonair prince. Drama, starring Dorothy McGuire, Maggie McNamara, Jean Peters, Clifton Webb, Louis Jourdan and Rossano Brazzi(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether an extra hour a week in nature improves your health, and lain Gregoire investigates an illness that can transform a child's personality overnight. Meanwhile, Giles Yeo investigates whether trendy plant-based milks are any healthier than dairy, and Guddi Singh witnesses pioneering cancer surgery(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Michael Baggott and Anita Manning assess items at the RAF Museum in Hendon, north London. Presenter Paul Martin explores the history of one of Britain's premier art schools(n)

programme.title: 10 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Documentary following young dogs and their new owners over the course of their all-important first six months together. The first episode features Chihuahua Chloe, setting up residence in an east London nail salon. She immediately wants to befriend 10-year-old Rocky, formerly top dog in the household, but Rocky is not interested. Meanwhile, border collie Jura joins a ski patroller in the highlands of Scotland, rescue pup Pedro arrives in the Surrey countryside from Spain and golden retriever Lola joins Hampshire family the Payes(n)

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Footage of animals that have made their homes in urban environments around the world. Featuring huge colonies of bats in Adelaide, smooth-coated otters at home in Singapore, and reticulated pythons living on the streets of Bangkok. Narrated by Daniel Kaluuya(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon take part in a gruelling efficiency test in a bid to prove their worth, but end up mistakenly attacking a tea lady with onions from Jones's van. Classic comedy first shown in 1973, guest starring Fulton Mackay (Porridge)(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: The next leg of Michael Portillo's journey begins in Skagway, Alaska, where he learns about writer Jack London, one of the gold prospectors who hiked to the Klondike 100 years ago. He then crosses into Canada on the White Pass and Yukon railway. Along the way he is invited to shake his tail feathers in a grouse dance, heads for the home of the Vancouver Giants, tries his hand at ice hockey and visits the Canadian Motion Picture Park(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition of the quiz as Sandi Toksvig indulges in a bit of 'Quaffing' and 'Scoffing', inviting Jo Brand, Phill Jupitus and Prue Leith to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Mock the Week chairman Dara O Briain comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by stand-up stars Felicity Ward and Mo Gilligan(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin knows Teddy is connected to the government, so he needs new banking arrangements that are not under Teddy's control. He turns to his well-connected former nemesis Avi. Teddy and Julia make contact with their man in Costa Rica - but they need to earn his trust before he will begin helping them(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Teddy learns that Gustavo and Mariela are not so easily controlled as he thought, and he has another body to bury and yet more lies to tell. Louie gives Manboy an ultimatum(n)

programme.title: While We're Young (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary film-maker and his wife are flattered by the attention of a young fan and his other half, a friendship that has an unexpected impact on their lives. Comedy drama, starring Ben Stiller, Naomi Watts, Adam Driver and Amanda Seyfried(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke transform a dank Victorian garden in Sunderland into an inviting space for the owner's grandchildren(n)

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Gardeners' World presenter Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow salad ingredients in the garden, including tomatoes, lettuce and cucumbers, but a blight hampers proceedings. She then prepares lime-leaf salad, dandelion fritters and nettle beer(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countryfile presenters Adam Henson and Steve Brown team up with antique experts David Harper and Roo Irvine for a trip around the rolling Cotswold Hills, hoping to find items that will make the biggest profit at auction. The competing duo also explore the area's history, with Adam discovering the vital work a charity played saving horses during the Great War and Steve taking a trip down a canal to learn how a little boat save hundreds of lives at Dunkirk(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Dungeness in Kent, where Margherita Taylor meets people behind a campaign to save film director Derek Jarman's famous cottage and garden and spends time with the last of the hardy women who helped launch lifeboats by hand in former times. Matt Baker explores the town's strange and barren landscape and chats to people harvesting sloes for a very special gin festival. Guest presenter Deborah Meaden looks at what the future holds for rural post offices(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Scientist Andrea Turkalo examines the social bonds formed by elephants deep in the rainforests of central Africa. After more than 20 years of field study, she has begun to unravel the secrets of the animals' lives and the meanings of the sounds they use to communicate with each other. Richard Armitage narrates(n)

programme.title: Flatpack Empire
programme.desc: Cameras go behind the scenes of IKEA, one of the world's most successful, enigmatic and recognisable global brands. It begins with a look at how the company is starting to open up its design headquarters to outside influences. British designer Tom Dixon has been invited to collaborate on a new bed-sofa, but it proves a source of disagreement(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin explore bold and extraordinary homes in Spain, a country renowned for experimental and fearless architecture. They include a metal home in the foothills of the Pyrenees that is more like a piece of art, an architect's home in Madrid made of seven vast concrete and steel beams and a space-age circular building in the wilderness of Aragon(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates(n)

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar becomes the prime suspect for a series of robberies carried out in his own distinctive style, and sets out to clear his name by catching the copycat crook responsible. His quest takes him to the Cote d'Azur, where he encounters the demure daughter of a wealthy holiday-maker, and before long the two are drawn together. Alfred Hitchcock's Oscar-winning romantic mystery, starring Cary Grant, Grace Kelly and Jessie Royce Landis(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrel and Catherine Southon find intricately carved walnuts and a silver eperne at Bletchley Park, Buckinghamshire, while Paul Martin meets Second World War codebreakers(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The explorer sets out to become the first person to reach the summit of a mountain in Greenland's Arctic wilderness. This already dangerous expedition is made all the more treacherous by the impact of climate change, as rising temperatures in the Arctic cause the ice sheets to melt. He must also brave rivers of freezing meltwater, quicksand, and treacherous glacial ice(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Women's downhill skiing action from the World Cup meeting at Garmisch Partenkirchen. Plus, a look at snowboarding group the No Medals Crew as they film their latest movie(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris are sent on a high-altitude mountain expedition across Peru in second-hand American cars. Plus, back at the track, Chris tests out the latest electric car from Porsche(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: As winter draws in and the weather grows wilder, the remaining couples are challenged to clear a path through a dense area of the wilderness for a dog sled team in just three hours. Once the work is completed, the captain of the sled team selects one of the couples to visit Ose Mountain, where they must clear debris from a reservoir and learn how to correctly butcher an animal in the wild(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores how Scotland's landscape has shaped the nation's history. He begins on a trek from Aberdeen to Oban, meeting a hermit called Jake and exploring his very unique treehouse. He then visits the community of Findhorn, finding out how this remote area of barren sand dunes became transformed into a thriving village whose main aim is to be environmentally, economically and socially self-sustaining. Frankie also views one of the most stunning glens in the Highlands, giving his perspective on the Glencoe massacre of 1692, before going deep underground to visit the Cruachan Power Station(n)

programme.title: Nightcrawler (2014)
programme.desc: A cynical petty crook is inspired when he sees a freelance camera crew, and decides to go into business filming crime scenes and accidents, then selling the footage to news broadcasters. His determination to stay ahead of the competition leads him down a dark path as his methods become increasingly ruthless. Crime drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Rene Russo and Bill Paxton(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Anthea Turner present the edition first shown on March 2, with performances by Living in a Box, Sam Brown, Jason Donovan, Tyree featuring Kool Rock Steady, Depeche Mode, Deacon Blue, Donna Summer, Wasp, Gloria Estefan, Texas, Simple Minds and Poison(n)

programme.title: Doris Day: Virgin Territory
programme.desc: A profile of the American singer, actress and animal rights activist, featuring contributions by James Garner, Richard Carpenter, Terence Davies and Don Pippin. The programme delves into the troubled private life and career highs and lows of the star of such films as Pillow Talk, Calamity Jane, That Touch of Mink, The Thrill of It All and Caprice. Narrated by Michael Brandon(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Lenny Henry host the edition first shown on March 9, featuring performances by the Reynolds Girls, Dusty Springfield, Womack & Womack, Deacon Blue, Paula Abdul, Bananarama and Lananeeneenoonoo, Donna Summer, Jason Donovan and Wasp(n)

programme.title: There's Only One Madonna
programme.desc: An examination of the singer's popularity in Britain, revealing her influence on the worlds of fashion, music and gender politics and following a group of fans on her tour. A known Anglophile, she is described as an inspiration to a generation of women, the founder of 'girl power' and an icon for the gay community. Contributors include Jean Paul Gaultier, Mel C, Britney Spears and Kylie Minogue. First shown in 2001(n)

programme.title: Madonna Rebel Heart Tour
programme.desc: Directors Danny B Tull and Nathan Rissman showcase the American pop singer's 2015-2016 world tour via as-live footage and candid behind-the-scenes coverage from the various venues that played host to the exuberant Queen of Pop. Includes performances of Madonna's most recent hits, along with classic tunes from throughout her decades-long career, such as Living for Love, Material Girl, Holiday, and an acoustic version of Like a Prayer(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Concert footage, home movies and interviews in Jamaica give a background to the Wailers' LP Catch a Fire, which launched Bob Marley onto the global music scene. Record label boss Chris Blackwell reveals how he masterminded the overdubbing of the album for the rock market and Rabbit Brundrick (keyboards) and Wayne Perkins (electric guitar) tell how they were brought back in to add their parts to the songs(n)

programme.title: Reginald D Hunter's Songs of the South
programme.desc: The comedian concludes his road trip through the American South by exploring the musical heritage of Mississippi and Louisiana. In Memphis, he meets songwriting partners Steve Cropper and Eddie Floyd at Stax Records, before going on the trail of the origins of both delta and hill country blues in the company of musicians Seasick Steve and Cedric Burnside. His final port of call is New Orleans, where he meets Dr John, Irma Thomas and Allen Toussaint, three of the city's most celebrated artists(n)

programme.title: Doris Day: Virgin Territory
programme.desc: A profile of the American singer, actress and animal rights activist, featuring contributions by James Garner, Richard Carpenter, Terence Davies and Don Pippin. The programme delves into the troubled private life and career highs and lows of the star of such films as Pillow Talk, Calamity Jane, That Touch of Mink, The Thrill of It All and Caprice. Narrated by Michael Brandon(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Meerkat pups Squirt and Weeny belong to one of the most famous animal families on the planet and are also part of a 20-year animal behaviour study. David Attenborough narrates the full story of the research, from the early days when it was a challenge to even get close to a meerkat to the latest revelations about these animals and their complex lives(n)

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: A diary dating from 1943 is discovered hidden in an old bunker and Montalbano is entrusted with the dark secrets revealed in the diary and starts to research the time around which it was written. During his investigation, a 90-year-old businessman is found murdered in his own home and the detective starts to think that the events cannot possibly be unrelated. Italian crime drama, starring Luca Zingaretti(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Rome
programme.desc: Clive spends time in the Italian capital as he goes in search of the dolce vita in the eternal cityand tries to acquire some Roman sophistication. During his stay, he meets a socialite prince and Mussolini's son and learns a lesson from a sexologist(n)

programme.title: The Other Side of Hope (2017)
programme.desc: Khaled arrives in Helsinki from Syria and applies for asylum, then begins trying to find his sister, who he believes is still alive. On the other side of town, travelling salesman Waldemar Wikstrom packs up his things and sets out to fulfil his dream of owning a restaurant and soon crosses paths with the refugee. Aki Kaurismaki's comedy drama, starring Sakari Kuosmanen and Sherwan Haji. In Finnish(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Anthea Turner present the edition first shown on March 2, with performances by Living in a Box, Sam Brown, Jason Donovan, Tyree featuring Kool Rock Steady, Depeche Mode, Deacon Blue, Donna Summer, Wasp, Gloria Estefan, Texas, Simple Minds and Poison(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Lenny Henry host the edition first shown on March 9, featuring performances by the Reynolds Girls, Dusty Springfield, Womack & Womack, Deacon Blue, Paula Abdul, Bananarama and Lananeeneenoonoo, Donna Summer, Jason Donovan and Wasp(n)

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks
programme.desc: Hollywood composers including Hans Zimmer, James Cameron, Danny Elfman, John Williams, Quincy Jones and Trent Reznor combine to give a privileged look inside the musical challenges and creative secrecy of the film score(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, featuring Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Brighton & Hove Albion at Damson Park(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the area around the summit of Snowdon in summer, finding buzzards nesting and moths being hunted by migrant nightjars from Africa. He also climbs Cadair Idris, the highest peak in the south part of the park, visits sand dunes full of orchids and spends a day on the moors watching the hen harrier.(n)

programme.title: Attenborough: 60 Years in the Wild
programme.desc: David Attenborough reflects on his TV career, charting changes in the natural world and mankind's understanding of the planet as well as developments in wildlife film-making. In the first episode, he looks back on the animal encounters that have shaped his career - from the early black-and-white Zoo Quest days to a close encounter with a lion pride in the pitch dark. He also shares a few tricks of the trade, such as how to catch a Komodo dragon and the secret to maintaining continuity in the most difficult circumstances, and recalls the making of landmark series Life on Earth(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Picasso is known as the godfather of modern art. Art historian David Dibosa explores six decades of BBC archives to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Dibosa reveals how filmmakers have portrayed the child genius who drew like a master, and the adult who rejected his conventional talent in a quest to reflect the world in ways that shattered all the rules(n)

programme.title: Picasso's Last Stand
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the last decade of the great artist's life, through the memories of family, close friends and the people he allowed into his world in the 1960s. As his health declined in these final years, Picasso faced damaging criticism of his work for the first time, and intimate details about his bohemian lifestyle were revealed. Yet, in the midst of disaster, he rediscovered his revolutionary spirit with a creative surge that produced some of his most sexually frank and comic work(n)

programme.title: It's True, It's True, It's True: Artemisia on Trial
programme.desc: Historical drama based on original court transcripts, that recreates the trial in 1612 of Agostino Tassi, an Italian Baroque artist who was accused of the rape of painter Artemisia Gentileschi. In order to defend himself, Tassi questioned his victim's morality, rubbished the evidence against him and nobbled the witnesses, all tactics that mirror those that are still used sometimes today. Breach Theatre's critically accalimed play blends history, myth and contemporary commentary to ask whether much has really changed in the last four centuries(n)

programme.desc: Linda is convinced Mick and Shirley are plotting against her, so she sneaks out and confides her fears to Sharon, who urges the troubled landlady to fight for her marriage. Karen realises Bernadette will not let her suspicions about Keanu drop, so she takes a risky move. The reality of Daniel's situation dawns on him(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 400m gold medallist Christine Ohuruogu, Cricket World Cup-winner Mark Wood, Sheffield United striker Billy Sharp and Great Britain swimming star Fran Halsall join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: When Murphy falls ill and has to recuperate in Glyn's bed on doctor's orders, a war between the two men ensues. Comedy, starring Robert Pugh, Steve Spiers, Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Alexandria Riley(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Hollywood star Mark Ruffalo, who is currently appearing in new legal thriller Dark Waters. Friends star David Schwimmer and British comedian and writer Nick Mohammed discuss their new spy comedy Intelligence. Episodes star Tamsin Greig also appears to talk about her role in Julian Fellowes' new period drama, Belgravia. Plus, there's music from singer-songwriter Alicia Keys, who performs her new single Underdog, while more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Winkle gatecrashes Gary's plans for a family day out at an indoor mini golf course in celebration of Big Gary's birthday. Meanwhile, Denise takes a shine to the crockery in the restaurant and Terri sticks her oar into Gary's friend John the Butcher's date. She knows that John is already seeing one of her friends, so righteously informs the date that the man she is having dinner with is a 'class A wrong un'. Comedy, starring Tom Davis, Laura Checkley and Camille Coduri(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The remaining queens are split into two teams for a girl group battle. With the help of Grammy-nominated singer and producer MNEK, the two newly formed groups record vocals and perform their own versions of the same song. Little Mix's Jade Thirlwall joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Shivi Ramoutar, and a special guest faces his or her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: After a positive first couple of weeks, the temptation of unhealthy treats and alcohol is threatening to undo the group's progress, so Tom shares a series of recipes to help the volunteers with their temptations for unhealthy treats. He starts with his own take on Asian crispy duck pancakes, before demonstrating how to make fudge chocolate brownies which are lower in calories. Later, Tom visits Lauren in her home, where he gives her a lesson in making prawn and avocado rice paper rolls which contain lots of fresh, healthy ingredients(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by comedian Ardal O'Hanlon and Wales star Gareth Anscombe as they review the opening Six Nations matches and look ahead to the weekend's fixtures. Plus, Wales, England, Ireland and Scotland will battle it out in a series of challenges to see who can be crowned the Sin Bin Showdown champion(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from Newark with experts Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell. While in town, Charlie pays a visit to the National Civil War Centre(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Bradley Stoke, Bristol, to come to the horticultural aid of Laura and Andrew, who require a water-saving, wildlife-friendly space(n)

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). John Inverdale presents all the action from Murrayfield, where both teams are looking to recover from respective defeats against Ireland and France. Scotland could count themselves somewhat unfortunate to return from Dublin on the losing side, but England's dismal start against France cost them any chance of victory. The sides shared the spoils in a thrilling 38-38 draw when they met in the final match of last season's competition. The Scots staged an improbable fightback from 31-7 down at half-time, with only George Ford's try and conversion in the final play of the game denying them a first win at Twickenham in 36 years, though the draw was enough for them to retain the Calcutta Cup following their 25-13 win here in 2018. With analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott, Andy Nicol and John Barclay, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Chris Paterson(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining 10 acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie struggles to deal with recent events in the emergency department, and Connie finds it increasingly difficult to hide her emotions, which could bring her and Archie closer together. Meanwhile, Rash finds himself in the middle of an intense and complex case when two friends are brought to the emergency department for treatment(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him and he locks himself away in a cupboard, so it is up to Agnes, Father Quinn and best man Buster to try to calm him down. Meanwhile, Rory's secret is revealed to his mother. Bawdy comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Paddy Houlihan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: Free State of Jones (2016)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting for the Confederacy during the American Civil War grows disillusioned by the rebel cause and deserts, returning to his Mississippi home. He unites a force of struggling farmers and escaped slaves into a militia, fighting to turn their county into an independent state, free from the rule of both sides. Fact-based drama, starring Matthew McConaughey and Gugu Mbatha-Raw(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August.
