programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: James Williams presents the latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience at Oasis Academy MediaCityUK in Salford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, his wife Erin and their two children sample life in Melbourne for a week, hoping the move would improve their work-life balance and quality of family time(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks at faith and autism, meeting a minister with Asperger's syndrome and hearing from members of the world's first performing autistic adult choir(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes property hunting in rural Wiltshire with a couple who have £950,000 to buy a family home with land, where they and their young children can enjoy a self-sufficient lifestyle. Jules also takes time out to find out about the county's glove-making heritage and sees some famous examples made for famous figures(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Italy (Kick-off 3.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the concluding match from the second round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. The hosts will be in confident mood after their impressive performance against England in their opening match, while Italy were comfortably routed by Wales. The French prevailed 25-14 in Italy last year to extend their winning run against the Azzurri in this competition to six matches. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede, Sergio Parisse and Joe Worsley, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Paul O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering how to look good. It's a story of bodybuilding, beauty queens and leather bikinis, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: From ancient Syria to present day Sheffield and out into the wilds of space, something is stalking the Doctor and her friends. As Graham, Yaz and Ryan return home to see friends and family, they find themselves haunted by very different experiences. Who is the figure calling from beyond the stars for help, and what are the fearsome Chagaskas terrorising Aleppo in 1380? To find the answers, the Doctor and her companions must embark on a mission that forces them to face their darkest fears(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: At the Turners, Shelagh receives a phone call from the Hong Kong adoption agency, which has the alarming news that someone from May's past has arrived in the UK and wants to see the little girl. Meanwhile, Valerie's cousin Maureen is heavily pregnant and hoping her baby will be born on Thursday, the same date as her father and grandfather. However, her due date isn't so soon and keen to bring on the birth, Maureen takes a big risk by secretly taking castor oil - with potentially disastrous consequences(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Widowed antique dealer Mark Easterbrook investigates when his name appears on a list found in the shoe of a dead woman, and his enquiries lead him to the home of three spinsters in Much Deeping. The village is a place of old traditions, macabre rituals and strange beliefs, a place of witches, curses and spells, and as more deaths occur Mark starts to fear for his own life and sanity. First of a two-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Sheffield United v Bournemouth at Bramall Lane. West Ham were looking to avenge a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of the Citizens on the opening weekend of the season, while Sheffield United were aiming to continue their impressive campaign back in the top flight by inflicting more misery upon the struggling Cherries(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, featuring Everton v Liverpool at Goodison Park, Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Brighton & Hove Albion at Damson Park(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the second round of fixtures, featuring France v Italy at Stade Beaublanc, Scotland v England at Scotstoun and Ireland v Wales at Energia Park(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The accounts worker at a cancer hospital who siphoned off £642,000 of NHS money into personal bank accounts. Plus, the GP practice manager who faked e-mails to award himself a pay rise. Presented by Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Brompton in Kent, Workington in Cumbria and Great Barr in the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicola West and Jake Andrews dream of giving their little daughter Auri the best start in life they can, even if it means leaving the UK. However, Nicola is scared of hurting her loved ones by taking her daughter far away. Nicki Chapman offers the pair a week in Brisbane to check out the homes, jobs and lifestyles on offer, to help them decide whether to make a move once and for all(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers uncover a disturbing trade in counterfeit airbags that would fail to work in the event of a crash. They also crack down on rogue landlords in a dawn raid(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion head to the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the aid of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also learns about Captain Matthew Webb, the first person to swim the English Channel(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Digital freelancer Rosa is so busy trying to make ends meet she risks harming her daughter - and herself. Elsewhere, the identity of Joe's secret new online is unclear(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A simple missing person's case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre where it's clear that no one is what they seem. Simon Williams and Josie Lawrence guest star(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life on the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Highland cattle farmers Simon and Emma show their finest bull, Merlin, and dream of winning big at the Great Yorkshire Show. In the Shorthorn category, Stuart and Gail take a big step by showing their livestock for the first time. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: There's a 1970s theme prior to Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram's last auction in Cambridge, as Raj weighs in with a large set of standing scales and Natasha shells out on a lazy Susan. Natasha also steps aboard a narrowboat antiques shop moored on a canal, while Raj rummages in the den of a pair of foxes and emerges with perhaps his most unpredictable purchase to date(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Dev Griffin host usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers are in Stoke-on-Trent to help a young couple who have run out of ideas when it comes to their garden, having sorted their new-build home. With only three fences, a shed and a few weeds to work with, the designers look to transform this characterless plot into the chic 'outdoor room' the couple dream of(n)

programme.desc: The quarter-finalists prepare for another two challenges but first there's a surprise as, for one week only, Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge joins the judges in Angela Hartnett's place. programme.title: Sportscene Live
programme.desc: BSC Glasgow v Hibernian (Kick-off 12.00pm). Rob Maclean presents coverage of the fifth-round tie from The Indodrill Stadium in Alloa. programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Sheffield United v Bournemouth at Bramall Lane. West Ham were looking to avenge a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of the Citizens on the opening weekend of the season, while Sheffield United were aiming to continue their impressive campaign back in the top flight by inflicting more misery upon the struggling Cherries(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from today's Scottish Cup fifth-round fixtures, featuring BSC Glasgow v Hibernian at The Indodrill Stadium and Clyde v Celtic at Broadwood Stadium(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The accounts worker at a cancer hospital who siphoned off £642,000 of NHS money into personal bank accounts. Plus, the GP practice manager who faked e-mails to award himself a pay rise. Presented by Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Brompton in Kent, Workington in Cumbria and Great Barr in the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicola West and Jake Andrews dream of giving their little daughter Auri the best start in life they can, even if it means leaving the UK. However, Nicola is scared of hurting her loved ones by taking her daughter far away. Nicki Chapman offers the pair a week in Brisbane to check out the homes, jobs and lifestyles on offer, to help them decide whether to make a move once and for all(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers uncover a disturbing trade in counterfeit airbags that would fail to work in the event of a crash. They also crack down on rogue landlords in a dawn raid(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion head to the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the aid of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also learns about Captain Matthew Webb, the first person to swim the English Channel(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Digital freelancer Rosa is so busy trying to make ends meet she risks harming her daughter - and herself. Elsewhere, the identity of Joe's secret new online is unclear(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A simple missing person's case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre where it's clear that no one is what they seem. Simon Williams and Josie Lawrence guest star(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life on the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Highland cattle farmers Simon and Emma show their finest bull, Merlin, and dream of winning big at the Great Yorkshire Show. In the Shorthorn category, Stuart and Gail take a big step by showing their livestock for the first time. Narrated by Bill Paterson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: There's a 1970s theme prior to Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram's last auction in Cambridge, as Raj weighs in with a large set of standing scales and Natasha shells out on a lazy Susan. Natasha also steps aboard a narrowboat antiques shop moored on a canal, while Raj rummages in the den of a pair of foxes and emerges with perhaps his most unpredictable purchase to date(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Dev Griffin host usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers are in Stoke-on-Trent to help a young couple who have run out of ideas when it comes to their garden, having sorted their new-build home. With only three fences, a shed and a few weeds to work with, the designers look to transform this characterless plot into the chic 'outdoor room' the couple dream of(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is convinced Mick and Shirley are plotting against her, so she sneaks out and confides her fears to Sharon, who urges the troubled landlady to fight for her marriage. Karen realises Bernadette will not let her suspicions about Keanu drop, so she takes a risky move. The reality of Daniel's situation dawns on him(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 400m gold medallist Christine Ohuruogu, Cricket World Cup-winner Mark Wood, Sheffield United striker Billy Sharp and Great Britain swimming star Fran Halsall join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Winkle gatecrashes Gary's plans for a family day out at an indoor mini golf course in celebration of Big Gary's birthday. Meanwhile, Denise takes a shine to the crockery in the restaurant and Terri sticks her oar into Gary's friend John the Butcher's date. She knows that John is already seeing one of her friends, so righteously informs the date that the man she is having dinner with is a 'class A wrong un'. Comedy, starring Tom Davis, Laura Checkley and Camille Coduri(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets Hollywood star Mark Ruffalo, who is currently appearing in new legal thriller Dark Waters. Friends star David Schwimmer and British comedian and writer Nick Mohammed discuss their new spy comedy Intelligence. Episodes star Tamsin Greig also appears to talk about her role in Julian Fellowes' new period drama, Belgravia. Plus, there's music from singer-songwriter Alicia Keys, who performs her new single Underdog, while more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The remaining queens are split into two teams for a girl group battle. With the help of Grammy-nominated singer and producer MNEK, the two newly formed groups record vocals and perform their own versions of the same song. Little Mix's Jade Thirlwall joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Bird and Shivi Ramoutar, and a special guest faces his or her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: After a positive first couple of weeks, the temptation of unhealthy treats and alcohol is threatening to undo the group's progress, so Tom shares a series of recipes to help the volunteers with their temptations for unhealthy treats. He starts with his own take on Asian crispy duck pancakes, before demonstrating how to make fudge chocolate brownies which are lower in calories. Later, Tom visits Lauren in her home, where he gives her a lesson in making prawn and avocado rice paper rolls which contain lots of fresh, healthy ingredients(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from Newark with experts Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell. While in town, Charlie pays a visit to the National Civil War Centre(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists prepare for another two challenges but first there's a surprise as, for one week only, Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge joins the judges in Angela Hartnett's place. Then it's down to business as Mary Berry asks them to cook their ultimate family pie and Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish using a root vegetable usually served on the side. As usual, those who least impress the judges compete in the eliminator, a recipe close to Tom's heart that he expects to be executed perfectly. Presented by Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). John Inverdale presents all the action from Murrayfield, where both teams are looking to recover from respective defeats against Ireland and France. Scotland could count themselves somewhat unfortunate to return from Dublin on the losing side, but England's dismal start against France cost them any chance of victory. The sides shared the spoils in a thrilling 38-38 draw when they met in the final match of last season's competition. The Scots staged an improbable fightback from 31-7 down at half-time, with only George Ford's try and conversion in the final play of the game denying them a first win at Twickenham in 36 years, though the draw was enough for them to retain the Calcutta Cup following their 25-13 win here in 2018. With analysis from Martin Johnson, Jeremy Guscott, Andy Nicol and John Barclay, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Chris Paterson(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining 10 acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie struggles to deal with recent events in the emergency department, and Connie finds it increasingly difficult to hide her emotions, which could bring her and Archie closer together. Meanwhile, Rash finds himself in the middle of an intense and complex case when two friends are brought to the emergency department for treatment(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him and he locks himself away in a cupboard, so it is up to Agnes, Father Quinn and best man Buster to try to calm him down. Meanwhile, Rory's secret is revealed to his mother. Bawdy comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll and Paddy Houlihan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: Free State of Jones (2016)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting for the Confederacy during the American Civil War grows disillusioned by the rebel cause and deserts, returning to his Mississippi home. He unites a force of struggling farmers and escaped slaves into a militia, fighting to turn their county into an independent state, free from the rule of both sides. Fact-based drama, starring Matthew McConaughey and Gugu Mbatha-Raw(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures - Everton v Crystal Palace at Goodison Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Watford at AMEX Stadium. Everton were unbeaten in their last nine meetings with the Eagles, but the sides could not be separated when they met at Selhurst Park in August. Brighton recorded a comfortable 3-0 away win in the reverse fixture against the Hornets, but Watford had shown signs of improvement under Nigel Pearson(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience at Oasis Academy MediaCityUK in Salford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, his wife Erin and their two children sample life in Melbourne for a week, hoping the move would improve their work-life balance and quality of family time(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks at faith and autism, meeting a minister with Asperger's syndrome and hearing from members of the world's first performing autistic adult choir(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes property hunting in rural Wiltshire with a couple who have £950,000 to buy a family home with land, where they and their young children can enjoy a self-sufficient lifestyle. Jules also takes time out to find out about the county's glove-making heritage and sees some famous examples made for famous figures(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Italy (Kick-off 3.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the concluding match from the second round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. The hosts will be in confident mood after their impressive performance against England in their opening match, while Italy were comfortably routed by Wales. The French prevailed 25-14 in Italy last year to extend their winning run against the Azzurri in this competition to six matches. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede, Sergio Parisse and Joe Worsley, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Paul O'Connell(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Charlotte Smith and Steve Brown head to the Auchlyne Estate near Killin in Perthshire, the first of four visits they will be making over the next year to chart life there throughout the seasons. Plus, Tom Heap goes back to the classroom to see if the next generation of farmers are being given the training they need(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: From ancient Syria to present day Sheffield and out into the wilds of space, something is stalking the Doctor and her friends. As Graham, Yaz and Ryan return home to see friends and family, they find themselves haunted by very different experiences. Who is the figure calling from beyond the stars for help, and what are the fearsome Chagaskas terrorising Aleppo in 1380? To find the answers, the Doctor and her companions must embark on a mission that forces them to face their darkest fears(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: At the Turners, Shelagh receives a phone call from the Hong Kong adoption agency, which has the alarming news that someone from May's past has arrived in the UK and wants to see the little girl. Meanwhile, Valerie's cousin Maureen is heavily pregnant and hoping her baby will be born on Thursday, the same date as her father and grandfather. However, her due date isn't so soon and keen to bring on the birth, Maureen takes a big risk by secretly taking castor oil - with potentially disastrous consequences(n)

programme.title: The Pale Horse
programme.desc: Widowed antique dealer Mark Easterbrook investigates when his name appears on a list found in the shoe of a dead woman, and his enquiries lead him to the home of three spinsters in Much Deeping. The village is a place of old traditions, macabre rituals and strange beliefs, a place of witches, curses and spells, and as more deaths occur Mark starts to fear for his own life and sanity. First of a two-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1961 novel, starring Rufus Sewell, Sean Pertwee and Kaya Scodelario(n)

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.desc: Belle is intrigued to learn something, Wendy is tempted after a discovery, and Laurel makes a decision(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: In the 10,000th episode, Rita is surprised when a parcel arrives containing Dennis's ashes and a note requesting she scatter them in Blackpool. Jenny books a coach and invites many of the residents to join them, but the trip is not without incident. By the end of the jaunt, lives will have changed and one resident realises the time has come to leave the cobbles for pastures new(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia arrives in the Dodecanese, a far-flung group of islands at the gateway between Europe and the East. On the largest. Rhodes, she uncovers a treasure of trove of Byzantine art and has masterclass in painting historic frescoes. Travelling inland to the mountain village of Apollona, she meets some female entrepreneurs producing an age-old superfood made from honey and sesame. Julia's trip ends with a visit to the neighbouring island of Symi where, after sampling the local shrimps, she receives a lesson in the ancient and dangerous skill of sponge diving(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Keen to unwind, Geordie and Will head to a charity boxing match at Vic's gym. However, as Matthew and Luke face off in the ring the bout takes an unexpected twist. Will wonders if he's misjudged Matthew all along and when two boys are later found near death, Will must face up to his own part in the tragedy to help uncover the truth. Meanwhile, Leonard tries to reunite the warring Chapmans, who seem determined never to see eye-to-eye again. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Volunteers test two more diets - Eat Yourself Healthy, the latest spin on gut health, and The Complete Pegan Diet for Beginners, a new catchphrase combining paleo and vegan approaches. How will they rank on the leaderboard? Ranj Singh looks at the return of fibre, finding out whether it is worth jumping on the bandwagon, and Sian Williams look at the latest veg boxes on the market which use mis-shapen produce before it ends up as waste(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Wales (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the opening match in the second round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. Both sides were victorious last weekend, with Wales easily defeating a poor Italian outfit, and Ireland somewhat fortunate to edge out Scotland on this ground. Ireland prevailed 37-27 when the sides last met here in this competition en route to the Grand Slam in 2018. However, the Welsh emulated that feat last year, securing the title with a 25-7 victory over today's opponents at Principality Stadium. Gareth Anscombe kicked 20 points on that occasion to ensure former head coach Warren Gatland ended the campaign as the only man to guide a nation to three Grand Slams. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Gareth Thomas and Jonny Wilkinson, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Gordon D'Arcy and Shane Williams(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking former professional boxer Tony Bellew, singer and fashion designer Kelly Osbourne and BBC Breakfast presenter Dan Walker to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the semi-final of the celebrity singing contest. Duck, Fox, Hedgehog, Octopus and Queen Bee are the five remaining singers and this week two more will be unmasked and eliminated, leaving only three to go through to next week's final. Ken Jeong returns to the panel, joining regulars Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall in trying to guess the famous faces are behind the costumes(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the penultimate round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' will.i.am, Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Meghan Trainor seek out the nation's best vocal talent. The coveted spaces on the coaches' teams are filling up and as ever, there is a simple twist to the auditions - with the four coaches selecting their teams without being able to see them, looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: The Nutty Professor (1996)
programme.desc: An obese, terminally shy professor invents a potion that transforms him into a slim, loud-mouthed charmer. His new streamlined self immediately sets out to woo the woman of his dreams - but soon starts to ride roughshod over everything he used to care about. Remake of the 1963 Jerry Lewis comedy, starring Eddie Murphy, Jada Pinkett Smith, James Coburn and Larry Miller(n)

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: Keith takes funnyman Rob Beckett to a toy store and hunts for vintage clothes with his friend and former Celebrity Juice sparring partner Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: Rebound
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the quickfire quiz that tests the general knowledge of six contestants, who go head to head in a bid to walk away with a big cash prize. The players must give as many correct answers as they can in the time it takes the 'Rebound Bar' to move across the studio, with the money on offer rising accordingly(n)

programme.desc: Po and the students encounter a gang who want to fight Po, which leads them to discovering Jindiao's evil plan.(n)

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of Grenada, where he prepares pork with sweet potato mash and nutmeg cream sauce, and discovers why the spice is so important to the island. He also visits a rum distillery powered by a water wheel and makes a cocktail using a local chocolate liquor, and gets introduced to Grenada's national dish by chef Brian Benjamin(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up their Thai 'no fish' cakes and demonstrate how to turn meat-free sausages into smoky chorizo for a piri piri traybake. Michelin-starred chef Alexis Gauthier shows how he has transformed a classic French pate into a vegan 'faux gras', there are ideas for healthy smoothies and, for dessert, no-egg mini banoffee meringues with cashew cream and caramel sauce(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia arrives in the Dodecanese, a far-flung group of islands at the gateway between Europe and the East. On the largest. Rhodes, she uncovers a treasure of trove of Byzantine art and has masterclass in painting historic frescoes. Travelling inland to the mountain village of Apollona, she meets some female entrepreneurs producing an age-old superfood made from honey and sesame. Julia's trip ends with a visit to the neighbouring island of Symi where, after sampling the local shrimps, she receives a lesson in the ancient and dangerous skill of sponge diving(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: The penultimate round of the blind auditions in the spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' will.i.am, Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Meghan Trainor seek out the nation's best vocal talent. The coveted spaces on the coaches' teams are filling up and as ever, there is a simple twist to the auditions - with the four coaches selecting their teams without being able to see them, looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the semi-final of the celebrity singing contest. Duck, Fox, Hedgehog, Octopus and Queen Bee are the five remaining singers and this week two more will be unmasked and eliminated, leaving only three to go through to next week's final. Ken Jeong returns to the panel, joining regulars Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall in trying to guess the famous faces are behind the costumes(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets (2016)
programme.desc: When rivalry leaves them cast adrift on the streets of New York, two bickering hounds must put their differences aside and pull together. Animated adventure from the makers of Despicable Me, featuring the voices of Louis CK, Eric Stonestreet and Kevin Hart(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Ruth Davidson, Mark King, Ferne McCann and Dan Walker pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners take to the arena once more, performing routines based on a selection of different dance style. Kevin Kilbane won't be among them, having lost out in last week's skate-off to H. Which of tonight's celebrities will find themselves in the bottom two? Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As Morse sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera, Thursday and the team are faced with a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath. Back in Oxford, Morse meets a kindred spirit, while a ground-breaking college project becomes a fatal battle of the sexes. Shaun Evans stars as the cerebral detective, in an episode he also directed, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser and Sean Rigby(n)

programme.title: The Food and Drink Awards in Association with Staysure and thefoodawards.com
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts this event from the Merchant Taylors' Hall in London celebrating the UK's position as one of the world's leaders in food and drink, which has risen to an all-time high in the past few years. Awards include best organic producer, best family run restaurant and outstanding achievement, while contributors include Jeni Barnett, Andi Oliver, Linda Robson, Rosemary Shrager and Tom Read Wilson(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley is on the mend following his rodeo injury and determined to get back behind the wheel of the RV. As the pair continue on their journey, Barney is thrilled to be celebrating his birthday with a trip to the iconic Niagara Falls. Later, Bradley and Barney take the RV on a small cargo ferry as they head to Vermont. Here, the pair have a go at ice climbing, which proves treacherous for them and the crew, before a spot of dog sledding through the frozen wilderness and an inevitable race through the forests(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the second round of fixtures, featuring France v Italy at Stade de France, Ireland v Wales at Aviva Stadium and Scotland v England at Murrayfield(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase

programme.desc: Belle is intrigued to learn something, Wendy is tempted after a discovery, and Laurel makes a decision(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: In the 10,000th episode, Rita is surprised when a parcel arrives containing Dennis's ashes and a note requesting she scatter them in Blackpool. Jenny books a coach and invites many of the residents to join them, but the trip is not without incident. By the end of the jaunt, lives will have changed and one resident realises the time has come to leave the cobbles for pastures new(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia arrives in the Dodecanese, a far-flung group of islands at the gateway between Europe and the East. On the largest. Rhodes, she uncovers a treasure of trove of Byzantine art and has masterclass in painting historic frescoes. Travelling inland to the mountain village of Apollona, she meets some female entrepreneurs producing an age-old superfood made from honey and sesame. Julia's trip ends with a visit to the neighbouring island of Symi where, after sampling the local shrimps, she receives a lesson in the ancient and dangerous skill of sponge diving(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Keen to unwind, Geordie and Will head to a charity boxing match at Vic's gym. However, as Matthew and Luke face off in the ring the bout takes an unexpected twist. Will wonders if he's misjudged Matthew all along and when two boys are later found near death, Will must face up to his own part in the tragedy to help uncover the truth. Meanwhile, Leonard tries to reunite the warring Chapmans, who seem determined never to see eye-to-eye again. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: A chance encounter gives Pete a boost, while Jenny faces fresh heartbreak and Karen attempts to make amends with her mother. David starts his new venture, but soon realises that all is not as it seems. John Thomson, James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst star in Mike Bullen's long-running comedy drama(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform fairy-tale routines, hoping to get a high enough score from the judges and viewers to keep them out of the bottom two. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.desc: Actress and former glamour model Linda Lusardi takes on the diners, who are hoping to end their run on a high point after suffering a turbulent week. Will it be a photo-finish for the ex-beauty queen or will her opponents grab the cash? Presented by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up crispy party poppers with barbecue dipping sauce - ideal for movie nights - a breakfast burrito, aubergine curry and banana bread doughnuts. They are also joined by Prue Leith, who tucks into their mushroom wellington(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Volunteers test two more diets - Eat Yourself Healthy, the latest spin on gut health, and The Complete Pegan Diet for Beginners, a new catchphrase combining paleo and vegan approaches. How will they rank on the leaderboard? Ranj Singh looks at the return of fibre, finding out whether it is worth jumping on the bandwagon, and Sian Williams look at the latest veg boxes on the market which use mis-shapen produce before it ends up as waste(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Wales (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the opening match in the second round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. Both sides were victorious last weekend, with Wales easily defeating a poor Italian outfit, and Ireland somewhat fortunate to edge out Scotland on this ground. Ireland prevailed 37-27 when the sides last met here in this competition en route to the Grand Slam in 2018. However, the Welsh emulated that feat last year, securing the title with a 25-7 victory over today's opponents at Principality Stadium. Gareth Anscombe kicked 20 points on that occasion to ensure former head coach Warren Gatland ended the campaign as the only man to guide a nation to three Grand Slams. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Gareth Thomas and Jonny Wilkinson, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Gordon D'Arcy and Shane Williams(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase

programme.desc: Peter suffers kidney failure after drinking kerosene and is told he needs a transplant. Luckily, Brian is a match and offers one of his, but concerns are raised that a canine organ might not be suitable. Meanwhile, Meg and Chris collaborate on a poem for an important visitor(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger's one-night stand with a fellow alien he and Francine find in the woods develops into a full-blown relationship. However, he soon gets tired of his new girlfriend's quirks - and tries to get rid of her by calling the CIA. With the voice of Kim Kardashian(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A broke Hayley gets an office job and falls for a cute colleague, prompting her to consider embarking on an affair while on a work retreat to Belize. Meanwhile, Stan chauffeurs Steve and Francine to a mother-son dance, but things turn ugly when he refuses to let them dance together(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Rallo and his friends Bernard and Theodore perform in the school talent show, but their rap about fiscal responsibility proves unpopular with the student body. Meanwhile, Cleveland turns to daytime TV for answers when his own debut show is panned by critics. With the voices of Will.i.am and T-Pain(n)

programme.desc: Tash feels awkward as she realises she is starting to have feelings for Jaden, and Head rep David decides to treat himself(n)

programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Sarah Keyworth, Jonah Ray, Seann Walsh and London Hughes are reconstructed as sketches(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Miller's old Army captain shows up to make him a business proposition, which he turns down without regret. programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.desc: Raquel tries to comfort Curly and sends him to the Job Centre. Charlie returns and he is desperate to speak to Bet. Andy celebrates getting a place on a Combined Studies course(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The residents of Corfu are excited at the prospect of an imminent official visit from the king of Greece, and the Durrells are keen to get themselves involved with the welcoming committee. Leslie takes over leadership of a scout troop, but finds the children under his care far more of a challenge than expected. However, his match-making scheme seems to be going far more smoothly, as his mother and Spiros grow closer. Love may also be in the air for Margo as she makes her return home to the island(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Despite Rob's efforts to keep his wedding secret, his sisters enlist the help of the community to organise a surprise reception as the policeman prepares to marry Helen. Drama, with Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is abuzz with activity for the preparation of Janice and Penhale's wedding, but a medical issue could prevent Penhale from making it down the aisle. Meanwhile, Al is struggling with the demands of being best man, especially when Bert's marketing antics mean there is only whisky available at the pre-wedding party. Martin Clunes and John Marquez star(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa search for a nursery now that Janice has left, and Mrs Tishell is shocked to find out that Al has very high blood pressure. Louisa also helps with the diagnosis of a pupil, which spurs her on to explore the possibilities of a change of career. This is not welcome news for Martin, who dislikes any kind of change, even if it could be for the better. Elsewhere, Ruth is approached about selling her farm. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz, Selina Cadell and Ian McNeice(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The hunt for the killer of a seemingly innocent family man brings Ronnie into contact with his old boss, former DI Natalie Chandler, whose father is accused of the crime. She wastes no time in persuading the detective to go above and beyond the call of duty to exonerate her dad and find the real culprit - even if that means getting him into trouble with an increasingly frustrated Wes. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Harriet Walter, Martin Jarvis and Roy Hudd(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff recruits Yasmeen to assist in his magic act. Roy and Carla realise that Nina is homeless. A parcel arrives for Rita that inspires an impromptu trip to Blackpool(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Following the brutal murder of a gay man, Sam and Ronnie embark on a desperate mission to track down the victim's adoptive son, who has gone missing. The prime suspect soon emerges as the child's biological father - an alleged violent homophobe unable to stomach the idea of the youngster being brought up in a same-sex household - but was he acting alone? Guest starring Glynis Barber, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama starring Bradley Walsh. Ronnie and new partner Joe lead the investigation into the death of jeweller Harry Bernstein, who has been found with his hands and teeth missing. His wife, her lover and a former business associate are all possible suspects, but the case takes a surprising turn when the victim's sister receives a gruesome delivery. She's on the jury for the trial of a drug dealer and murderer who Ronnie has been trying to nail for years, and the threat to her points to jury tampering, so Jake and Kate have to take a dramatic approach to win the case. The problem is, they're up against a formidable defence barrister. Co-starring Ben Bailey Smith, Dominic Rowan and Georgia Taylor, with guests Helen Baxendale and Christopher Fulford(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the fatal stabbing of a psychiatrist with a caseload of violent adolescent patients, Ronnie and Joe's first instinct is to question those he was treating. However, the case takes a disturbing turn when the detectives discover a hidden sleeping compartment in the victim's office which has clearly been used recently by one of his underage visitors - and evidence comes to light suggesting he may have been having an affair with one of them. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Ramon Tikaram and Hattie Morahan(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old Deborah Ozturk has spent years longing to find her birth mother and thank her for the sacrifice she made when she gave her up as a baby. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Ray Jones from West Sussex realised as an adult that there was a void in his life, after he was separated from his mother and younger brother at the age of two. However, during his search for his family the previous year, he accessed his case file and is now haunted by a missed opportunity. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby decides to express repentance for his numerous sins, at the urging of his latest lodger, a religious student. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Don Warrington and Frances de la Tour(n)

With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Life on board with the 1,400 crew who live and work for up to nine months a year on luxury cruise ship the Regal Princess. As an 11-day voyage from Copenhagen to St Petersburg gets under way, some of the guests are vying for the award of most travelled passenger, while new dancer Dulcie is hoping to make the grade as part of the entertainment team. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Ed Perrin has been given permission to sail his vessel all the way into Stockholm through its stunning archipelago. It's a terrifying task for the skipper, for if he gets it wrong he could cause untold damage to a nearby historic windmill or even run aground. Injury strikes the entertainment team, leaving dancer Dulcie paired with a new recruit who doesn't know the routines, and a safety helmet causes problems for excursions agent Timothy and his perfectly styled hair. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley travels back to the country of her birth, travelling the length and breadth of India to explore its diverse landscapes and cultural traditions. In this episode, her epic journey begins in Tamil Nadu, where she witnesses religious ceremonies in glorious temples, before visiting Gangtok in Sikkim, the place where her mother lived as a child(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby encounters new challenges as a blistering heatwave hits Kerala, while Lydia decides she is ready for her first solo night shift. Return of the medical drama set in southern Indian, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital

Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is abuzz with activity for the preparation of Janice and Penhale's wedding, but a medical issue could prevent Penhale from making it down the aisle. Meanwhile, Al is struggling with the demands of being best man, especially when Bert's marketing antics mean there is only whisky available at the pre-wedding party. Martin Clunes and John Marquez star(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa search for a nursery now that Janice has left, and Mrs Tishell is shocked to find out that Al has very high blood pressure. Louisa also helps with the diagnosis of a pupil, which spurs her on to explore the possibilities of a change of career. This is not welcome news for Martin, who dislikes any kind of change, even if it could be for the better. Elsewhere, Ruth is approached about selling her farm. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz, Selina Cadell and Ian McNeice(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The hunt for the killer of a seemingly innocent family man brings Ronnie into contact with his old boss, former DI Natalie Chandler, whose father is accused of the crime. She wastes no time in persuading the detective to go above and beyond the call of duty to exonerate her dad and find the real culprit - even if that means getting him into trouble with an increasingly frustrated Wes. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Harriet Walter, Martin Jarvis and Roy Hudd(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff recruits Yasmeen to assist in his magic act. Roy and Carla realise that Nina is homeless. A parcel arrives for Rita that inspires an impromptu trip to Blackpool(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Following the brutal murder of a gay man, Sam and Ronnie embark on a desperate mission to track down the victim's adoptive son, who has gone missing. The prime suspect soon emerges as the child's biological father - an alleged violent homophobe unable to stomach the idea of the youngster being brought up in a same-sex household - but was he acting alone? Guest starring Glynis Barber, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama starring Bradley Walsh. Ronnie and new partner Joe lead the investigation into the death of jeweller Harry Bernstein, who has been found with his hands and teeth missing. His wife, her lover and a former business associate are all possible suspects, but the case takes a surprising turn when the victim's sister receives a gruesome delivery. She's on the jury for the trial of a drug dealer and murderer who Ronnie has been trying to nail for years, and the threat to her points to jury tampering, so Jake and Kate have to take a dramatic approach to win the case. The problem is, they're up against a formidable defence barrister. Co-starring Ben Bailey Smith, Dominic Rowan and Georgia Taylor, with guests Helen Baxendale and Christopher Fulford(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the fatal stabbing of a psychiatrist with a caseload of violent adolescent patients, Ronnie and Joe's first instinct is to question those he was treating. However, the case takes a disturbing turn when the detectives discover a hidden sleeping compartment in the victim's office which has clearly been used recently by one of his underage visitors - and evidence comes to light suggesting he may have been having an affair with one of them. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Ramon Tikaram and Hattie Morahan(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old Deborah Ozturk has spent years longing to find her birth mother and thank her for the sacrifice she made when she gave her up as a baby. Meanwhile, 74-year-old Ray Jones from West Sussex realised as an adult that there was a void in his life, after he was separated from his mother and younger brother at the age of two. However, during his search for his family the previous year, he accessed his case file and is now haunted by a missed opportunity. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby decides to express repentance for his numerous sins, at the urging of his latest lodger, a religious student. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Don Warrington and Frances de la Tour(n)

With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Life on board with the 1,400 crew who live and work for up to nine months a year on luxury cruise ship the Regal Princess. As an 11-day voyage from Copenhagen to St Petersburg gets under way, some of the guests are vying for the award of most travelled passenger, while new dancer Dulcie is hoping to make the grade as part of the entertainment team. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Ed Perrin has been given permission to sail his vessel all the way into Stockholm through its stunning archipelago. It's a terrifying task for the skipper, for if he gets it wrong he could cause untold damage to a nearby historic windmill or even run aground. Injury strikes the entertainment team, leaving dancer Dulcie paired with a new recruit who doesn't know the routines, and a safety helmet causes problems for excursions agent Timothy and his perfectly styled hair. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley travels back to the country of her birth, travelling the length and breadth of India to explore its diverse landscapes and cultural traditions. In this episode, her epic journey begins in Tamil Nadu, where she witnesses religious ceremonies in glorious temples, before visiting Gangtok in Sikkim, the place where her mother lived as a child(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby encounters new challenges as a blistering heatwave hits Kerala, while Lydia decides she is ready for her first solo night shift. But when a storm hits Barco, Ruby faces an agonising test that stretches her medical skills to the limits. Return of the medical drama set in southern Indian, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle spring into action when East German agents blackmail a government employee into revealing secret plans for a new type of jet-engine tank. Featuring an appearance by playwright Lynda La Plante, alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In Fiji's South Pacific paradise, people are coming under attack. Plunging off the beaten track into the remote mountains of the island's interior, freshwater detective Jeremy battles tropical storms and unforgiving jungles in an all-out search for victims, clues, and culprits. Colourful stories of monstrous fish are woven into the local folklore, but how much of this is fanciful, and how much of it is real?(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade takes on Africa's wild Zambezi River to find an unknown predator dragging fishermen to their deaths. Crocodiles and hippos are guilty of killing dozens of victims in plain view, yet it is said there is another monstrous beast stalking these deep and turbulent waters. Locals speak of 'the Mazunda' - but what is this sinister, child-swallowing creature?(n)

programme.title: Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day five of the first of three tournaments in the Coral Series, held at The Centaur in Cheltenham, featuring the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames. Ali Carter and Judd Trump were the victorious players at this stage last year, with Carter thrashing Xiao Guodong 6-0 and Trump edging past Barry Hawkins 6-5. Trump would go on to lift the trophy after defeating Carter 10-6 in the showpiece match, becoming the first player to win the tournament more than once since its inception in 2015. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)

programme.desc: Charles Butcher, Jason Lewis and Thomas Hird are in South Africa in search of sharks, hoping to lure these predators close enough to their boat to catch and tag them. programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A captured British spy is released when his Russian counterpart falls into British hands. C15 is tasked with ensuring his safety as well as establishing whether his information is genuine. However, such intentions fail miserably when he is poisoned. Bodie and Doyle set out to determine what part his daughter, a concert pianist, played in his death - assuming she really is his daughter(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Hornblower's resourcefulness turns the attack on the Spanish-held fortress into a famous victory, but Buckland's treachery puts his life into further danger. Surviving a risky mission to blow up the fortifications and an attempt by the prisoners to take command of the Renown, the hero faces the death penalty for pushing Captain Sawyer into the hold - and hopes Archie's dying words can save him from the noose. Naval adventure, based on the stories by CS Forester, and starring Ioan Gruffudd(n)

programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to the rivers of India's Himalayan region to investigate a fresh spate of attacks by a type of catfish, but his plans to catch a specimen run into trouble when locals reveal it is sacred and he will be cursed if it is touched. programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In Fiji's South Pacific paradise, people are coming under attack. Plunging off the beaten track into the remote mountains of the island's interior, freshwater detective Jeremy battles tropical storms and unforgiving jungles in an all-out search for victims, clues, and culprits. Colourful stories of monstrous fish are woven into the local folklore, but how much of this is fanciful, and how much of it is real?(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade takes on Africa's wild Zambezi River to find an unknown predator dragging fishermen to their deaths. Crocodiles and hippos are guilty of killing dozens of victims in plain view, yet it is said there is another monstrous beast stalking these deep and turbulent waters. Locals speak of 'the Mazunda' - but what is this sinister, child-swallowing creature?(n)

programme.title: Snooker: World Grand Prix
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day five of the first of three tournaments in the Coral Series, held at The Centaur in Cheltenham, featuring the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames. Ali Carter and Judd Trump were the victorious players at this stage last year, with Carter thrashing Xiao Guodong 6-0 and Trump edging past Barry Hawkins 6-5. Trump would go on to lift the trophy after defeating Carter 10-6 in the showpiece match, becoming the first player to win the tournament more than once since its inception in 2015. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)
